pageDescription: Discover conferences, workshops, webinars, and expos shaping the cosmetics industry.

seo.title: Events | Cosmetech
seo.description: Upcoming and past cosmetics industry events from Cosmetech.

title: Cosmetech Formulation Summit 2026
summary: An intensive summit connecting formulation scientists, R&D leaders, and ingredient innovators across beauty and personal care.

children.text: The Cosmetech Formulation Summit is the region's premier gathering for cosmetic scientists and R&D professionals. Over eight days, attendees will explore cutting-edge advances in ingredient science, stability testing, and sustainable formulation practices.
Who Should Attend

This summit is designed for senior formulation chemists, R&D directors, ingredient suppliers, and product developers looking to stay ahead of regulatory and consumer-driven changes in the beauty landscape.
Key Themes

Sessions will cover biobased actives, microbiome-friendly formulation, waterless beauty, and the integration of AI-assisted stability modelling. A dedicated track on APAC regulatory harmonisation will run throughout the week.

agenda.label: Opening keynote: The next decade of cosmetic science
Panel: Biobased actives — sourcing, efficacy, and claims
Workshop: Stability testing under tropical conditions
Roundtable: AI in formulation — promise vs. practice

title: Fragrance and Sensory Forum
summary: A two-day industry forum focused on fragrance creation, sensory science, and consumer testing in modern beauty products.

children.text: The Fragrance and Sensory Forum brings together perfumers, sensory scientists, and brand teams for two immersive days of workshops and case studies centred on the intersection of scent, emotion, and product experience.
Workshop Format
Each session is limited to 40 participants to ensure hands-on engagement. Attendees will work directly with raw materials, conduct blind sensory evaluations, and present findings to peer groups.
Topics Covered

Fragrance longevity and substantivity, consumer sensory panels, olfactory storytelling in brand identity, naturals vs. synthetics in Middle Eastern markets, and the regulatory landscape for fragrance allergen disclosure.

agenda.label: Keynote: Scent as a sensory brand language
Hands-on: Raw material evaluation and blind testing
Session: Regulatory updates on fragrance allergen disclosure
Closing panel: Where is fragrance science headed?

title: EuroCosmetech Conference 2026
summary: Europe's flagship cosmetics industry conference, bringing together formulators, brand leaders, and suppliers to explore the frontiers of beauty science.

children.text: EuroCosmetech is the definitive annual gathering for Europe's cosmetics industry. Three full days of keynotes, panels, and curated networking sessions give attendees unparalleled access to thought leaders shaping the regulatory and innovation agenda across the continent.
Conference Tracks

This year's programme runs across four parallel tracks: Ingredient Science, Sustainability & Packaging, Regulatory Affairs, and Digital Innovation. Each track features a mix of invited talks and open-submission presentations.
Exhibition Floor
Over 120 suppliers will exhibit across 3,000 sqm of floor space. Pre-scheduled one-to-one meetings can be booked through the conference portal in advance of the event.

agenda.label: Opening address: The European Green Deal and cosmetics
Track sessions begin (Ingredient Science / Sustainability / Regulatory / Digital)

title: Active Ingredients Masterclass 2026
summary: A hands-on two-day masterclass for cosmetic chemists and R&D professionals focused on the latest developments in active ingredient science.

children.text: This intensive two-day masterclass provides in-depth technical training on the science, formulation, and substantiation of active ingredients. Designed for practising cosmetic chemists, the programme balances theory with practical laboratory sessions.
Programme Overview

Day one covers peptide and growth-factor actives, ceramide complexes, and encapsulation technology for enhanced delivery. Day two focuses on antioxidant systems, SPF boosters, and building robust in-vitro and in-vivo claims packages.
Certification
Delegates who complete both days receive a certificate of attendance from the Cosmetic Chemists Society, eligible for CPD recognition under the Society of Cosmetic Scientists accreditation framework.

agenda.label: Peptide actives: mechanisms, stability, and formulation challenges
Lab session: Encapsulation techniques for actives

title: Sustainable Beauty Expo 2026
summary: A four-day expo showcasing the next generation of sustainable ingredients, packaging, and supply chain solutions for the global beauty industry.

children.text: The Sustainable Beauty Expo is Asia's largest dedicated platform for green beauty innovation. Across four days, visitors can explore exhibitor stands, attend curated conference sessions, and connect with the brands and suppliers redefining what sustainable beauty means in practice.
What to Expect

More than 200 exhibitors will present sustainable ingredient innovations, compostable and refillable packaging solutions, carbon-neutral manufacturing processes, and verified supply chain traceability tools.
Conference Programme
The co-located conference features 60 speakers across six themed summits including Biodiversity & Sourcing, Circular Packaging, Clean Chemistry, and Scope 3 Emissions in the beauty supply chain.

agenda.label: Opening keynote: Defining sustainability beyond claims
Panel: Circular packaging — what's actually working?

title: Digital Beauty and Retail Forum
summary: A virtual forum exploring how DTC brands, retailers, and technology partners are reshaping the beauty commerce experience.

children.text: The Digital Beauty and Retail Forum is a one-day virtual event exploring the fast-changing landscape of beauty commerce. Sessions will examine how brands are using data, personalisation, and digital channels to build loyalty and drive conversion in a crowded market.
Session Highlights

Key sessions include live commerce and social selling in Southeast Asia, first-party data strategies post-cookie, AI-powered product recommendation engines, and the evolving role of beauty retailers as content platforms.
Format

All sessions are streamed live and available on-demand for 30 days after the event. Registered attendees will also have access to the speaker slide deck library and a facilitated virtual networking lounge.

agenda.label: Keynote: The DTC beauty playbook in 2026
Panel: Live commerce — converting viewers to buyers

title: BeautyTech and AI Innovation Expo
summary: An expo where AI teams, product developers, and digital marketers explore practical innovation across the beauty value chain.

children.text: The BeautyTech and AI Innovation Expo connects technologists with beauty professionals across a three-day programme of demonstrations, talks, and hands-on lab sessions. From generative AI in product development to machine learning for demand forecasting, the expo covers the full spectrum of technology applications in beauty.
Innovation Showcases

Live technology showcases will include AI skin analysis tools, virtual try-on platforms, automated formulation software, and predictive trend analytics dashboards — all demonstrated in working product environments.
Startup Pitch Competition
Twenty early-stage beautytech startups will pitch to a panel of investors and brand executives. The winning team receives a six-month accelerator placement and a Rs 25 lakh innovation grant.

agenda.label: Expo doors open and exhibitor showcase begins
Opening keynote: AI as a creative collaborator in beauty
Demo stage: Live AI skin diagnostics demonstration
Panel: Responsible AI in consumer-facing beauty products

title: Regulatory and Compliance Webinar: APAC
summary: A practical webinar covering regulatory updates and compliance pathways for cosmetics teams operating in key APAC markets.

children.text: This focused half-day webinar is designed for regulatory affairs professionals and compliance teams managing product registrations, safety dossiers, and labelling requirements across the APAC region. Sessions are presented by practising regulatory consultants with direct market experience.
Markets Covered

Sessions will focus on updated notification frameworks in China (NMPA), India (BIS), ASEAN harmonisation under the ASEAN Cosmetic Directive, and recent changes to Japan's Pharmaceutical and Medical Device Act as it applies to quasi-drug products.
Who Should Join
Regulatory affairs managers, legal counsel, quality assurance leads, and product managers responsible for market access in Asia. A live Q&A with the presenting consultants closes the session.

agenda.label: China NMPA update: New notification categories and ingredient restrictions
India BIS: Mandatory certification requirements and timelines
ASEAN harmonisation: Where we are and what's next
Japan quasi-drug: Practical implications for skin-care brands
